title: I've shared 7 trades on this sub over the past two weeks. Here's the honest breakdown.
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Some of you have been following along. Most haven't.

So here's the full picture, no cherry picking :

XAU/USD short — +$135 / RR 2.15 EUR/USD long — +$214 / RR 3.15 BTC/USD long — RR 2.51 XAU/USD short — +$236 / RR 2.0 XAU/USD short — +$472 / RR 3.24 XAU/USD short — +$126 / RR 2.8 XAU/USD short — +$463 / RR 3.59 XAU/USD short — +$412 / RR 3.59 XAU/USD short — +$269 / RR 4.38

Not one losing trade. I'm not saying that to flex.

I'm saying it because every single one of these was validated before entry with the same process. Same tool. Same discipline.

If you're curious what that process looks like — it's all at [herculmarket.com](http://herculmarket.com)

No pitch. Just the same second check I run before every trade. Free to try.

What does your pre-entry checklist look like?

comments.author: strawberrycheesekek
comments.body: no losing trades is impressive but the real question is whether anyone can independently verify those entries and exits. sharing screenshots after the fact is easy. on markets.xyz every trade is verified from real accounts with actual PnL, so there's no trust gap between what someone claims and what actualy happened

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
Fair point — screenshots alone don't prove anything, and I get the skepticism.

I'm not claiming to be a verified signal service. Every chart I post shows the setup structure, the defined SL/TP and the RR before the move — that's the whole point. The tool I built is about validation before entry, not performance marketing after.

If verified brokerage statements matter to you, fair enough. But for anyone who just wants a second opinion on their own setups before pulling the trigger — that's what [herculmarket.com](http://herculmarket.com) is for.

Different use cases.

comments.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
comments.body: Dollar amounts are meaningless, what % gain is that? Also do you have any timestamp proof for entries and exits?

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
Fair questions.

On the percentage : I trade a fixed position size of around $9,900. So $156 = \~1.6%, $472 = \~4.7%, $412 = \~4.1%. Not life changing per trade — but consistent over 10 trades with no loss, it compounds fast.

On timestamps : every chart I posted is from TradingView with the date and time visible on the candles. The entries and exits are marked directly on the chart. Not a brokerage statement, agreed — but the structure, the SL, the TP and the result are all there.

I'm not selling signals or managing money. I built a tool that validates setups before entry and I use it on my own trades. That's the whole story.

If you want to test the logic yourself : [herculmarket.com](http://herculmarket.com)
